rejection_reasons: receiver caps rise above the individual stones and read as added smooth courses
the continuous receiver surfaces hide the C004 rubble silhouette
the base remains mechanically regular rather than source-conditioned
C004 reads as a third brick course instead of a rubble apron
the debris remains too evenly spaced and visually detached
receiver exposure still dominates the base transition
the lower receiver descends as a smooth skirt and suppresses the rubble
the base interfaces remain visually over-regular
the receiver-to-stone balance does not match the source
receiver vertical bands use flat authored gray and lose source stone detail
the base is geometrically improved but the exposed receiver treatment remains visible
magnified alpha review finds background wedges at the three module contacts
the C003/C004 transition still exposes literal background between rubble stones
all module contacts close, but raised receiver top caps show as flat dark-gray annular bands
the visible caps replace stone detail with artificial horizontal surfaces

acceptance_basis: all three declared module-contact bands contain zero background pixels
open-top inset receiver collars remain hidden behind the stone silhouettes
C002, C003, and C004 top proofs retain their measured ovoid ratios
C004 reads as 32 irregular stones in two staggered rows rather than a third masonry course
